channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: The Internet Archive Returns Online Amid Security Strengthening Efforts
item.description: The Internet Archive returns online after a cyberattack, with its Wayback Machine in provisional read-only format. Founder Brewster Kahle emphasizes the need for caution and prioritizes data safety over service availability.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Bursztynsky, Fast Company

item.title: Tech consultant goes on trial in death of Cash App founder Bob Lee
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — The tech consultant charged in Cash App founder Bob Lee's stabbing death had no motive to kill him and in fact was forced to defend himself against Lee, who had become aggressive while on a multiday drug bender, lawyers for Nima Momeni said in opening statements Monday.
item.dc:creator: JANIE HAR, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Apple accused of restricting workers' Slack, social media use
item.description: The National Labor Relations Board (NLRB) filed a complaint against Apple on Thursday, accusing the iPhone maker of improperly restricting workers’ use of Slack and other social media.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Bankinter explora vías de aumento de la eficiencia a través de la IA
item.description: Bankinter, de la mano de Microsoft, está implantando desde hace aproximadamente un año Microsoft 365 Copilot en diversas áreas del banco.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: In Denmark, 50 well-preserved Viking Age skeletons have been unearthed, a rare discovery
item.description: Archaeologists in Denmark have unearthed a Viking Age burial ground containing some 50 “exceptionally well-preserved” skeletons.
item.dc:creator: James Brooks, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Opinion: Musk’s courtship with MAGA has driven X into a misinformation abyss
item.description: If X is a town square, it's inundated with an uncontrolled, right-wing mob. And Musk is their cheering benefactor.
item.dc:creator: Lorraine Ali, The Mercury News

item.title: MoneyGram hack exposes customers' sensitive data, cripples services
item.description: MoneyGram, an American payment and money transfer platform, confirmed a data breach that exposed sensitive customer data to hackers, forcing its services offline for five days.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Pentagon releases final CMMC rule, paving way for implementation
item.description: The Pentagon has taken another big step forward to make CMMC a reality. DoD projects the requirements will start showing up in contracts by mid-2025. The post Pentagon releases final CMMC rule, paving way for implementation first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Justin Doubleday, Federal News Network

item.title: A whopping 342,817 Lego Technic pieces are used to build a sports car
item.description: Lego and McLaren, the British supercar manufacturer, teamed up to create a fully drivable, life-sized replica of the McLaren P1 hypercar.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Internet Archive data breach exposes more than 31 million user accounts: Reports
item.description: "Services are currently stopped to upgrade internal systems," Kahle wrote in a Thursday update. "We are working to restore services as quickly and safely as possible. Sorry for this disruption.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Jackson, NewsNation

item.title: TypeScript 5.7 improves error reporting
item.description: TypeScript 5.7, the latest planned release of Microsoft’s JavaScript variant with syntax for types, is now available as a beta release. With the update, the language’s type system is now able to report errors when variables have never been initialized.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: North Carolina maker of high-purity quartz back operating post-Helene
item.description: SPRUCE PINE, N.C. (AP) — One of the two companies that manufacture high-purity quartz used for making semiconductors and other high-tech products from mines in a western North Carolina community severely damaged by Hurricane Helene is operating again.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: OpenAI sees continued attempts to use AI models for election interference
item.description: OpenAI has seen continued attempts by cybercriminals to use its artificial intelligence (AI) models for fake content aimed at interfering with this year’s elections, the ChatGPT maker said in a new report.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: This privacy and compliance threat throws shade at iPhone Mirroring
item.description: Apple-focused IT admins using device management software should perhaps temporarily disable iPhone Mirroring across their device fleets to prevent inadvertent privacy or compliance challenges as a result of using the new macOS Sequoia feature. (Apple is supposed to be working on a fix already.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Scammers exploit grief with fake funeral streaming on Facebook
item.description: Scammers are offering video streaming for funeral services and taking advantage of people who are grieving to get credit card information.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Here’s what’s new in Office 2024
item.description: Microsoft has just released Office 2024, the new non-subscription version of its office suite. It’s available in three editions: Office Home 2024 for consumers, Office Home & Business 2024 for consumers and small businesses, and Office LTSC 2024 for midsize and large businesses.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Deno 2.0 arrives, ready to battle Node.js
item.description: Deno 2.0, a major update to the open source Deno runtime for JavaScript, TypeScript, and WebAssembly, is now available as a production release. It emphasizes backward compatibility with the rival Node.js runtime and NPM and a stabilized standard library.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Musk steps up Starlink access as Hurricane Milton approaches Florida
item.description: SpaceX and CEO Elon Musk ramped up the rollout of Starlink's direct-to-cell service for areas expected to be hit Wednesday by Hurricane Milton, marking the latest assistance efforts by the aerospace company.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: American Water cyberattack renews focus on protecting critical infrastructure
item.description: CAMDEN, N.J. (AP) — A cyberattack continues to affect the largest regulated water and wastewater utility company in the United States, renewing a focus on the importance of protecting critical infrastructure sites.
item.dc:creator: BRUCE SHIPKOWSKI, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: US considers asking court to break up Google as it weighs remedies in the antitrust case
item.description: The U.S. Department of Justice is considering asking a federal judge to break up Google after its ubiquitous search engine was declared an illegal monopoly, but it is just one of many possible remedies under review, according to a court filing.
item.dc:creator: SHAWN CHEN, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: JDK 24: The new features in Java 24
item.description: Java Development Kit (JDK) 23 having arrived September 17, work already has begun on JDK 24, with four features so far proposed for the release: a vector API, a class-file API, warnings to prepare developers for future restrictions on the use of JNI (Java Native Interface), and a late barrier expans
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: TikTok is designed to be addictive to kids and causes them harm, US states' lawsuits say
item.description: More than a dozen states and the District of Columbia filed lawsuits against TikTok on Tuesday, saying that the popular short-form video app is designed to be addictive to kids and harms their mental health.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO and BARBARA ORTUTAY, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: 2024 global network outage report and internet health check
item.description: The reliability of services delivered by ISPs, cloud providers and conferencing services is critical for enterprise organizations.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: California’s vetoed AI bill: Bullet dodged, but not for long
item.description: Artificial intelligence has the power to revolutionize industries, drive economic growth, and improve our quality of life. But like any powerful, widely available technology, AI also poses significant risks.
item.dc:creator: Info World
